hero.eyebrow: Optimize your practice. Accelerate your progress.
hero.sub: RiffRoutine is your personal rehearsal room. Upload your tabs, build routines, track your progress, and never practice aimlessly again.

card1.title: Immersive Player
card1.desc: Load any Guitar Pro or tab file and practice it in our player with built-in metronome, speed control, loop mode, and score visualization. Your rehearsal room in the browser.

card2.title: Smart Routines
card2.desc: Create practice routines with specific duration and target BPM per exercise. The automatic timer alerts you when it's time to move to the next one.

card3.title: BPM Tracking
card3.desc: Log your speed in every session and visualize how you improve over time. Percentage towards your goal, history, and comparison between sessions.

card4.title: Stats & Consistency
card4.desc: Yearly heat map, day streak, total time accumulated by technique, skills radar. Everything so you never lose track of your progress.

how.label: Process
how.step1Desc: Sign up for free in under 30 seconds. No credit card, no commitments. Just you and your guitar.
how.step2Desc: Import your Guitar Pro files, create your own exercises, and organize them in your personal library.
how.step3Desc: Launch a routine, follow the timer, and log your BPM. Your progress is saved automatically.

testimonials.label: Community
testimonials.t1Text: I practiced without structure for years. Since using RiffRoutine, I know exactly how much I've improved and what I need to work on.
testimonials.t2Text: The routine system is insane. I can play the tab while the timer and metronome run. All in one.
testimonials.t3Text: Watching your BPM go up week by week is addictive. I went from 90 to 145 BPM in alt picking in 3 months with real discipline.

cta.label: Ready?
cta.desc: Join the guitarists who practice with intention, track their progress, and improve every week.
cta.btn: Start for free — no card required

auth.signupSuccess: Check your inbox to confirm your account.

ionian.name: Ionian (Major)
ionian.desc: Natural major scale. Bright, happy, and resolute sound. The foundation of Western music.

dorian.name: Dorian
dorian.desc: Minor scale with a major 6th. Melancholic sound with a floating, bright touch. Classic in Jazz, Funk, and Blues rock.

phrygian.name: Phrygian
phrygian.desc: Dark, exotic sound with high tension due to the ♭2. Characteristic of Flamenco and widely used in Metal.

lydian.name: Lydian
lydian.desc: Major scale with an augmented 4th. Mysterious, spatial, dreamlike, and very open sound. Common in film scores.

mixolydian.name: Mixolydian
mixolydian.desc: Major scale with a minor 7th. Happy but with a characteristic tension. The quintessential scale for Blues, Rock, and Funk.

aeolian.name: Aeolian (Minor)
aeolian.desc: Natural minor scale. Sad, melancholic, or epic sound. The most used for minor progressions.

locrian.name: Locrian
locrian.desc: The most unstable and dissonant mode because it lacks a perfect 5th. Mostly used in Jazz and high-tension Metal passages.

blues.name: Blues
blues.desc: The minor pentatonic scale with the added "Blue Note" (♭5). Provides the characteristic gritty blues sound.

pentatonic-minor.name: Minor Pentatonic
pentatonic-minor.desc: The most popular scale in modern guitar. Five notes without semitones, making it extremely easy to improvise.

pentatonic-major.name: Major Pentatonic
pentatonic-major.desc: A bright five-note version. Country-like, happy, and sweet sound. The pillar of Country and Pop music.

harmonic-minor.name: Harmonic Minor
harmonic-minor.desc: Natural minor scale with a raised 7th to generate strong tension. Classical, Baroque, and Arabic sound.

harmonic-major.name: Harmonic Major
harmonic-major.desc: A rare and exotic scale that combines major third brightness with minor sixth darkness.

melodic-minor.name: Melodic Minor
melodic-minor.desc: Natural minor scale with major 6th and 7th. Very fluid and resolute, used in contemporary Jazz.

double-harmonic.name: Double Harmonic
double-harmonic.desc: Byzantine or Arabic sounding scale. Features two augmented seconds, making it very exotic.

hungarian.name: Hungarian
hungarian.desc: Gypsy minor scale. Essentially harmonic minor but with an augmented fourth (#4). Neoclassical metal sound.

persian.name: Persian
persian.desc: Very tense and semitone-rich sound. Strongly evokes Middle Eastern melodies.

neopolitan.name: Neapolitan
neopolitan.desc: Combines the dissonant start of the Phrygian scale (♭2) with the resolute brightness of the Major scale (7).

neopolitan-minor.name: Neapolitan Minor
neopolitan-minor.desc: Similar to harmonic minor but with the dark addition of a flat second (♭2). Gothic and dramatic sound.

diminished.name: Diminished
diminished.desc: Symmetrical octatonic scale alternating Tone-Semitone. Generates constant and infinite tension.

augmented.name: Augmented
augmented.desc: Hexatonic scale formed by overlapping whole tones. Evokes an unreal feeling of buoyancy.

m7-arpeggio.name: Maj7 Arpeggio
m7-arpeggio.desc: The 4 structural notes of a Major 7th chord. Perfect for sweeps in major progressions.

min7-arpeggio.name: m7 Arpeggio
min7-arpeggio.desc: The 4 structural notes of a minor 7th chord. Essential for sweep picking over minor chords.

dom7-arpeggio.name: Dominant Arpeggio
dom7-arpeggio.desc: Arpeggio of the dominant chord. Its unstable tritone interval creates a strong need to resolve.

major-triad.name: Major Triad
major-triad.desc: The basic major chord played linearly. Strong, bright, and static.

minor-triad.name: Minor Triad
minor-triad.desc: The basic minor chord played linearly. Dark and sad.

hirajoshi.name: Hirajōshi
hirajoshi.desc: Traditional Japanese pentatonic scale derived from Koto tuning. Melancholic and epic sound.

iwato.name: Iwato
iwato.desc: Extremely dissonant and dark oriental pentatonic scale, similar to a Locrian mode without a third.

kumoi.name: Kumoi
kumoi.desc: Japanese pentatonic variant (Sakura). Offers a ceremonial, ancient, and introspective tone.

hon-kumoi.name: Hon Kumoi-joshi
hon-kumoi.desc: Traditional scale based on dramatic intervallic leaps. Deep and reflective sound.

chinese.name: Chinese
chinese.desc: Based on traditional oriental tunings. Its structure avoids minor thirds and sixths.

meta.title: Privacy Policy
meta.description: How RiffRoutine collects, uses and protects your data

privacy.intro: At RiffRoutine, we take your privacy seriously. This policy explains what data we collect, how we use it, and what rights you have over it.

account.title: Account information
account.description: When you register, we collect your email address to create and manage your account.

files.title: Guitar Pro files
files.description: Files you upload (.gp, .gpx, .gp5, etc.) are stored securely to enable playback within the app. We do not analyse or share the content of your files.

progress.title: Practice progress and history
progress.description: We store statistics about your practice sessions (exercises completed, BPM progression, session duration) to show you your improvement over time.

technical.title: Technical data
technical.description: Basic usage data such as browser type, device, and approximate location (country level) collected anonymously for performance and error monitoring.

howWeUse.title: How We Use Your Data
items: Provide and maintain the RiffRoutine service
Display your personal practice statistics and progress history
Send transactional emails (account confirmation, password reset)
Improve app performance and fix bugs

dataStorage.title: Data Storage and Security
dataStorage.description: Your data is stored on secure servers provided by Supabase, located in the European Union. We apply industry-standard security measures including encryption in transit (TLS) and at rest. Uploaded files are stored in isolated, access-controlled buckets.

dataSharing.title: Data Sharing
dataSharing.description: We do not sell, rent or trade your personal data. We only share data with the following third-party service providers strictly necessary to operate RiffRoutine:

retention.title: Data Retention
retention.description: We retain your data for as long as your account is active. If you delete your account, all associated data — including uploaded files and practice history — will be permanently deleted within 30 days.

rights.title: Your Rights
rights.description: Under GDPR and applicable privacy laws, you have the right to:
items: Access the personal data we hold about you
Export your data in a portable format
rights.contact: To exercise any of these rights, contact us at {email}.

cookies.title: Cookies
cookies.description: RiffRoutine uses only essential cookies required for authentication and session management. We do not use tracking or advertising cookies.

changes.title: Changes to This Policy
changes.description: We may update this policy from time to time. We will notify you of significant changes via email or an in-app notice. Continued use of RiffRoutine after changes constitutes acceptance of the updated policy.

contact.title: Contact
contact.description: For any privacy-related questions, please contact us at {email}.

meta.title: Terms of Service
meta.description: Terms and conditions for using RiffRoutine

terms.intro: By creating an account or using RiffRoutine, you agree to these Terms of Service. Please read them carefully.

service.title: The Service
service.description: RiffRoutine is a web application designed to help guitarists improve their skills through structured practice routines, tablature playback, scale visualisation, and progress tracking.

account.title: Your Account
items: You must be at least 13 years old to use RiffRoutine.
You are responsible for maintaining the confidentiality of your account credentials.
You are responsible for all activity that occurs under your account.
You must provide a valid email address and keep it up to date.

content.title: User Content
content.description: You retain full ownership of the Guitar Pro files and any other content you upload. By uploading content, you grant RiffRoutine a limited licence to store and process that content solely for the purpose of providing the service to you. You confirm that you have the right to upload any files you submit and that doing so does not infringe any third-party rights.

acceptable.title: Acceptable Use
items: Use RiffRoutine for any unlawful purpose
Upload files containing malware or malicious code
Attempt to gain unauthorised access to any part of the service
Upload content that infringes copyright or other intellectual property rights

availability.title: Service Availability
availability.description: We strive to keep RiffRoutine available at all times but do not guarantee uninterrupted access. We may perform maintenance, updates or changes to the service at any time. We are not liable for any downtime or data loss caused by circumstances beyond our reasonable control.

termination.title: Termination
termination.description: You may delete your account at any time from your account settings. We reserve the right to suspend or terminate accounts that violate these Terms, with or without prior notice depending on the severity of the violation.

disclaimer.title: Disclaimer of Warranties
disclaimer.description: RiffRoutine is provided "as is" without warranties of any kind, express or implied. We do not warrant that the service will be error-free, uninterrupted, or meet your specific requirements.

liability.title: Limitation of Liability
liability.description: To the fullest extent permitted by law, RiffRoutine shall not be liable for any indirect, incidental, special or consequential damages arising from your use of the service.

changes.title: Changes to These Terms
changes.description: We may update these Terms from time to time. We will notify you of material changes via email or in-app notice. Continued use after changes constitutes acceptance.

contact.title: Contact
contact.description: For questions about these Terms, contact us at {email}.

meta.title: Contact
meta.description: Get in touch with the RiffRoutine team

hero.label: Support
hero.title: Contact Us
hero.subtitle: Have a question, found a bug, or want to suggest a feature? We'd love to hear from you.

message.label: Message
message.placeholder: Describe your question or issue in detail...

success.title: Message sent!
success.description: Thanks for reaching out. We'll get back to you within 48 hours.

form.error: Something went wrong. Please try again or email us directly at {email}.

alternative.title: Prefer email?
alternative.description: You can also reach us directly at

response.title: Response time
response.description: We typically respond within 48 hours on business days.

welcome.title: Welcome to RiffRoutine
welcome.description: Your personal guitar practice companion. Let's take a quick tour so you can start playing right away.

home.title: Your Dashboard
home.description: Here you'll see a summary of your progress: your practice streaks, time spent this week, and shortcuts to pick up exactly where you left off.

navbarLibrary.title: Exercise Library
navbarLibrary.description: Explore and organize all your exercises. Upload Guitar Pro files and access them anytime.

library-01.title: Create Your Content
library-01.description: Click the '+' button to add your first custom exercise and start building your own practice catalog.

library-02.title: Upload Your Tablature
library-02.description: You can drag and drop your .gp5 or .gp file here to view the interactive sheet music and practice with the built-in player.

library-03.title: Give It a Name
library-03.description: Type a title for your exercise. This will help you keep your library organized and find it quickly for your routines.

library-04.title: Categorize Your Technique
library-04.description: Assign tags like Alternate Picking or Sweep Picking to organize your library and filter your exercises by specific technique.

library-05.title: Define the Tempo
library-05.description: Set your current BPM and your final goal. This allows the tool to monitor your progress and adjust the metronome automatically.

library-06.title: Difficulty Level
library-06.description: Rate the challenge from 1 to 5 stars. This will let you filter your practice sessions based on your energy or technical level for the day.

library-07.title: Extra Notes
library-07.description: Add reminders about posture, fingering, or any technical detail you should keep in mind while practicing this exercise.

library-08.title: Your Turn!
library-08.description: Now that you know all the options, upload a .gp5 or .gp file, fill in the exercise details, and hit 'Save'. The tutorial will automatically continue once it's created.

library-09.title: Exercise Created!
library-09.description: Here is your new exercise. From now on, it will always appear in your library, ready to be practiced or added to a routine.

library-10.title: Time to Practice!
library-10.description: Click the 'Play' button to open the interactive player. This is where the magic happens: you can adjust speed, use the metronome, and follow the tab in real-time.

practice-01.title: Practice Panel
practice-01.description: Control your session from here: view the exercise name, adjust the BPM in real-time, and save your progress to update your stats when you finish.

practice-02.title: Musical Control Panel
practice-02.description: By loading a .gp5 file, you unlock the side panel. From here, you can mute tracks, change instruments, and adjust the metronome to fit your pace. Let's move on to routines.

navbarRoutines.title: Practice Routines
navbarRoutines.description: Create structured routines with your exercises. Set BPMs, target durations, and log every session.

routines-01.title: Design Your Workout
routines-01.description: Click '+' to create a new routine. Here you can group your exercises, define practice times, and organize your week to keep improving.

routines-02.title: Routine Details
routines-02.description: You can assign a name and a description to your routine. Organize them by goals, like 'Daily Warm-up' or 'Advanced Technique'.

routines-03.title: Add Your Exercises
routines-03.description: Use the selector to search your library and include the exercises you want to practice in this routine.

routines-04.title: Configure Your Routine
routines-04.description: Fill in the details and add at least two exercises to complete your first routine. You can assign a specific time to each one to organize your practice.

routines-05.title: Your Plan Is Ready
routines-05.description: Your new routine appears here. Now you have a structured workflow to make your practice sessions much more efficient.

routines-06.title: Start Your Session
routines-06.description: Press 'Play' to start your workout. The timer will keep track for you and alert you when it's time to move to the next exercise.

practice-03.title: Navigate Your Routine
practice-03.description: Use the exercise selector or the arrows to jump between different parts of your workout without leaving the player.

practice-04.title: Finish and Save
practice-04.description: Click this button to end your session. We'll record your practice time and progress so you can see your evolution in the stats panel.

practice-05.title: Session Summary
practice-05.description: Review your practice time and adjust the reached BPM before saving. This data will feed your progress charts and personal statistics.

practice-06.title: Mission Accomplished!
practice-06.description: Confirm the final data of your session. Upon saving, we'll update your practice streak and speed records on the dashboard. Let's head to the app catalog.

navbarExplore.title: Explore and Learn
navbarExplore.description: The theoretical heart of the app. Here you'll find our chord library, interactive scales, and improvisation tools designed to expand your musical vocabulary.

explore-01.title: Explore the Basics
explore-01.description: We've prepared a selection of essential exercises: from scales and chords to improvisation techniques. Use them for warming up or as a base for your own routines.

navbarStats.title: Track Your Progress
navbarStats.description: Every session is recorded. Watch your BPM and practice time grow week by week.

stats-01.title: Your Progress in Figures
stats-01.description: Take a look at your global indicators: total hours practiced, consecutive day streak, and your average BPM evolution. It's the best way to keep motivation high.

stats-02.title: Detailed Analysis
stats-02.description: Explore your progression charts and recent session history. Identify where you're excelling and which techniques need an extra boost.

finish.title: All Set!
finish.description: You've completed the tour! You now have the tools to create exercises, organize routines, and see your daily improvement. Time to rock that guitar!

SystemExercises.sys_composition_notes: Dedicated space for creating original ideas. Use this time to develop melodic motifs, chord progressions, or riffs from scratch.
SystemExercises.sys_improvisation_notes: Free improvisation. Test your licks, phrasing, and musicality without relying on a score.
SystemExercises.sys_scales_notes: Practice scales up and down using the metronome. Focus on the precision of both hands.
SystemExercises.sys_chords_notes: Master the fretboard by practicing chords and inversions in any position.
SystemExercises.sys_3_notes_beat_notes: Rhythmic exercise based on triplets. Perfect for developing timing in compound meters or blues and rock licks.
SystemExercises.sys_4_notes_beat_notes: Sixteenth note practice to improve speed and binary subdivision. Essential for fast passages and shredding.
SystemExercises.sys_3_strings_sweep_notes: Focus on a fluid, single-stroke picking motion. Ensure perfect synchronization with your fretting hand to prevent notes from bleeding into each other.
SystemExercises.sys_5_strings_sweep_notes: Control the pick hand's follow-through across all 5 strings. Subtle palm muting and fret-hand muting are vital to maintain clarity in each arpeggio shape.
SystemExercises.sys_tapping_ex01_notes: Focus on the impact strength of your tapping finger. The note should sound with the same intensity as a hammer-on from the fretting hand.
SystemExercises.sys_tapping_ex02_notes: Advanced exercise traversing all strings. Keep your tapping hand relaxed and ensure adjacent strings are muted to avoid unwanted noise.

CompositionPanel.title: Composition Center
CompositionPanel.notesPlaceholder: Write your lyrics here, song structure (Intro, Verse, Chorus), or arrangement ideas...

BecomeProModal.title: Limit Reached
BecomeProModal.routineLimit: The free plan allows a maximum of 3 routines. Upgrade to PRO to create unlimited routines and much more.
BecomeProModal.libraryLimit: The free plan allows creating a maximum of 10 custom exercises. Upgrade to PRO to create unlimited exercises.
BecomeProModal.statsLimit: Subscribe to Pro to unlock detailed 90-day analysis and your entire practice history.

Pro.headline: Unlock your full potential
Pro.subheadline: Pick the plan that fits your journey. Upgrade or cancel anytime.

free.description: Start practising for free. No credit card needed.

pro.description: Everything you need to build real habits and track your progress.

lifetime.description: Pay once, own it forever. All future Pro features included, price locked.

Pro.footer: Payments processed securely by Stripe. We never store your card details.

ProSuccess.headline: You're all set!
ProSuccess.subPro: Welcome to RiffRoutine Pro. Your subscription is active and all features are unlocked — go make some noise.
ProSuccess.subLifetime: Welcome to RiffRoutine Lifetime. You've secured unlimited access forever. Your price is locked, your features are all here.

perks.priceLocked: Price locked forever — no future increases
